title: oxalis fruticosa subsp. fruticosa
intro: Oxalis fruticosa subsp. fruticosa, also known as the Sourgrass or Wood-sorrel, is a perennial plant species in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of South Africa. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat digestive issues, urinary tract problems, and skin conditions due to its high concentration of oxalic acid and other compounds. The health benefits of Oxalis fruticosa subsp. fruticosa include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of diarrhea and dysentery. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and can also be applied topically as a poultice or ointment to treat various health conditions.
